notes: Use variant: DsButtonVariant.primary for ElevatedButton style

propertyMappings.transform: Use DsButtonVariant enum instead of ButtonStyle

beforeCode:
ElevatedButton(
  onPressed: () => submit(),
  child: Text('Submit'),
)
afterCode:
DsButton(
  variant: DsButtonVariant.primary,
  onPressed: () => submit(),
  child: Text('Submit'),
)

notes: Use variant: DsButtonVariant.secondary for OutlinedButton style

propertyMappings.transform: Use DsButtonVariant.secondary instead of ButtonStyle

beforeCode:
OutlinedButton(
  onPressed: () => cancel(),
  child: Text('Cancel'),
)
afterCode:
DsButton(
  variant: DsButtonVariant.secondary,
  onPressed: () => cancel(),
  child: Text('Cancel'),
)

notes: Use variant: DsButtonVariant.tertiary for TextButton style

propertyMappings.transform: Use DsButtonVariant.tertiary instead of ButtonStyle

beforeCode:
TextButton(
  onPressed: () => details(),
  child: Text('View details'),
)
afterCode:
DsButton(
  variant: DsButtonVariant.tertiary,
  onPressed: () => details(),
  child: Text('View details'),
)

notes: DsTextfield wraps input with built-in label and validation. Use DsField for more complex form layouts.

propertyMappings.transform: Label is a top-level prop, not nested in decoration
Use placeholder instead of hintText
Use errorMessage instead of errorText

beforeCode:
TextField(
  controller: _controller,
  decoration: InputDecoration(
    labelText: 'Email',
    hintText: 'Enter your email',
    errorText: _error,
  ),
  onChanged: (value) => validate(value),
)
afterCode:
DsTextfield(
  controller: _controller,
  label: 'Email',
  placeholder: 'Enter your email',
  errorMessage: _error,
  onChanged: (value) => validate(value),
)

notes: DsCheckbox includes its own label and optional description. No need for a separate CheckboxListTile.

propertyMappings.transform: Use indeterminate prop for tristate behavior

beforeCode:
Checkbox(
  value: _accepted,
  onChanged: (val) => setState(() => _accepted = val!),
)
afterCode:
DsCheckbox(
  value: _accepted,
  label: Text('I accept the terms'),
  description: Text('Please read the terms before accepting.'),
  onChanged: (val) => setState(() => _accepted = val),
)

notes: DsRadio includes its own label and optional description. Group multiple DsRadio widgets in a DsFieldset.
beforeCode:
Radio<String>(
  value: 'option1',
  groupValue: _selected,
  onChanged: (val) => setState(() => _selected = val!),
)
afterCode:
DsRadio(
  value: _selected == 'option1',
  label: Text('Option 1'),
  description: Text('Description for option 1'),
  onChanged: (_) => setState(() => _selected = 'option1'),
)

notes: DsSwitch includes its own label and optional description. Follows Designsystemet sizing and color tokens.

propertyMappings.transform: Use DsColor token instead of raw Color

beforeCode:
Switch(
  value: _darkMode,
  onChanged: (val) => setState(() => _darkMode = val),
)
afterCode:
DsSwitch(
  value: _darkMode,
  label: Text('Dark mode'),
  description: Text('Enable dark color scheme'),
  onChanged: (val) => setState(() => _darkMode = val),
)

notes: DsDialog uses a slot-based API with header, content, and footer sections.

propertyMappings.transform: Pass as String, not Widget
Use child slot for dialog body content
Use footer widget for action buttons
Not applicable; DsDialog follows Designsystemet styling

beforeCode:
showDialog(
  context: context,
  builder: (ctx) => AlertDialog(
    title: Text('Confirm'),
    content: Text('Are you sure?'),
    actions: [
      TextButton(onPressed: () => Navigator.pop(ctx), child: Text('Cancel')),
      ElevatedButton(onPressed: () => confirm(), child: Text('OK')),
    ],
  ),
)
afterCode:
showDialog(
  context: context,
  builder: (ctx) => DsDialog(
    title: 'Confirm',
    child: Text('Are you sure?'),
    footer: Row(
      children: [
        DsButton(
          variant: DsButtonVariant.secondary,
          onPressed: () => Navigator.pop(ctx),
          child: Text('Cancel'),
        ),
        DsButton(
          variant: DsButtonVariant.primary,
          onPressed: () => confirm(),
          child: Text('OK'),
        ),
      ],
    ),
  ),
)

notes: DsCard uses composable sub-components: DsCardHeader, DsCardBlock, DsCardFooter.

propertyMappings.transform: Wrap content in DsCardBlock for proper padding
Not applicable; DsCard uses Designsystemet shadow tokens
Not applicable; follows Designsystemet border radius
Use DsColor token instead of raw Color

notes: DsTabs combines TabBar and TabBarView into a single composable widget.

propertyMappings.transform: Use DsTab objects instead of Tab widgets
Renamed from onTap to onChanged
Not applicable; follows Designsystemet color tokens

beforeCode:
TabBar(
  controller: _tabController,
  tabs: [
    Tab(text: 'Tab 1'),
    Tab(text: 'Tab 2'),
    Tab(text: 'Tab 3'),
  ],
)
afterCode:
DsTabs(
  controller: _tabController,
  tabs: [
    DsTab(label: 'Tab 1'),
    DsTab(label: 'Tab 2'),
    DsTab(label: 'Tab 3'),
  ],
  onChanged: (index) => _tabController.animateTo(index),
)

notes: DsTooltip follows Designsystemet styling with proper contrast and spacing.

propertyMappings.transform: Renamed from message to content
Renamed from waitDuration to delay
Use DsTooltipPlacement enum instead of bool

beforeCode:
Tooltip(
  message: 'More information',
  child: Icon(Icons.info),
)
afterCode:
DsTooltip(
  content: 'More information',
  child: Icon(Icons.info),
)

notes: DsSelect provides a dropdown with built-in label, validation, and search support.

propertyMappings.transform: Use DsSelectOption list instead of DropdownMenuItem list
Use placeholder string instead of hint Widget

beforeCode:
DropdownButton<String>(
  value: _selectedCountry,
  hint: Text('Select a country'),
  items: countries.map((c) => DropdownMenuItem(
    value: c,
    child: Text(c),
  )).toList(),
  onChanged: (val) => setState(() => _selectedCountry = val),
)
afterCode:
DsSelect<String>(
  value: _selectedCountry,
  label: 'Country',
  placeholder: 'Select a country',
  options: countries.map((c) => DsSelectOption(
    value: c,
    label: c,
  )).toList(),
  onChanged: (val) => setState(() => _selectedCountry = val),
)

notes: DsChip supports removable and selectable variants through props.

propertyMappings.transform: Pass as String, not Widget
Renamed from onDeleted to onRemove
Use leading widget slot
Use DsColor token instead of raw Color

beforeCode:
Chip(
  label: Text('Flutter'),
  onDeleted: () => removeTag('Flutter'),
  avatar: Icon(Icons.code, size: 16),
)
afterCode:
DsChip(
  label: 'Flutter',
  onRemove: () => removeTag('Flutter'),
  leading: Icon(Icons.code, size: 16),
)

notes: DsBadge supports count, color, variant (base/tinted), maxCount, and positioning relative to a child.

propertyMappings.transform: Use count (int) for numeric badges
Use DsColor token instead of raw Color
Set count to null to hide the badge

beforeCode:
Badge(
  label: Text('3'),
  backgroundColor: Colors.red,
  child: Icon(Icons.notifications),
)
afterCode:
DsBadge(
  count: 3,
  color: DsColor.danger,
  variant: DsBadgeVariant.base,
  child: Icon(Icons.notifications),
)

notes: DsDivider follows Designsystemet spacing and color tokens.

propertyMappings.transform: Use DsSpacing token instead of raw double
Not applicable; follows Designsystemet border width
Use DsColor token instead of raw Color
Not supported; use padding on parent widget

beforeCode:
Divider(
  height: 24,
  thickness: 1,
  color: Colors.grey,
)

notes: DsSpinner replaces CircularProgressIndicator with Designsystemet styling and sizing.

propertyMappings.transform: DsSpinner is always indeterminate; for determinate progress, consider a custom approach
Use DsSpinnerSize enum (sm, md, lg) instead of strokeWidth
Use DsColor token instead of raw Color

beforeCode:
CircularProgressIndicator(
  strokeWidth: 3,
  color: Colors.blue,
)

notes: DsSkeleton provides a loading placeholder. For determinate progress bars, compose with DsSkeleton or use a custom widget.

propertyMappings.transform: DsSkeleton is a placeholder, not a progress bar; for loading states use DsSkeleton
Follows Designsystemet color tokens automatically
Follows Designsystemet color tokens automatically

beforeCode:
LinearProgressIndicator(
  value: null, // indeterminate
  backgroundColor: Colors.grey[200],
)
afterCode:
DsSkeleton(
  width: double.infinity,
  height: 8,
)

notes: DsList provides a structured list container. Individual items are composed with DsList.item or standard widgets.

propertyMappings.transform: Compose items as children of DsList
Include subtitle text within each list item
Include leading widget within each list item
Include trailing widget within each list item
Apply onTap to individual list items

beforeCode:
ListTile(
  leading: Icon(Icons.person),
  title: Text('John Doe'),
  subtitle: Text('john@example.com'),
  trailing: Icon(Icons.arrow_forward_ios),
  onTap: () => openProfile(),
)
afterCode:
DsList(
  children: [
    DsList.item(
      leading: Icon(Icons.person),
      title: 'John Doe',
      description: 'john@example.com',
      trailing: Icon(Icons.arrow_forward_ios),
      onTap: () => openProfile(),
    ),
  ],
)

notes: DsTable uses List<Widget> for columns and List<List<Widget>> for rows. Supports zebra striping and hover highlighting.

propertyMappings.transform: Use List<Widget> instead of DataColumn list
Use List<List<Widget>> instead of DataRow list

afterCode:
DsTable(
  columns: [Text('Name'), Text('Age')],
  rows: [
    [Text('Alice'), Text('30')],
  ],
  zebra: true,
  hover: true,
)

notes: DsSearch combines search input with suggestion dropdown. Use DsSuggestion for individual suggestion items.

propertyMappings.transform: Renamed from onSubmitted to onSubmit
Use placeholder instead of hintText
DsSearch includes a search icon by default
Set clearable: true for a clear button, instead of custom trailing widgets

beforeCode:
SearchBar(
  controller: _searchController,
  hintText: 'Search...',
  onChanged: (query) => performSearch(query),
  onSubmitted: (query) => submitSearch(query),
  leading: Icon(Icons.search),
  trailing: [IconButton(
    icon: Icon(Icons.clear),
    onPressed: () => _searchController.clear(),
  )],
)
afterCode:
DsSearch(
  controller: _searchController,
  placeholder: 'Search...',
  clearable: true,
  onChanged: (query) => performSearch(query),
  onSubmit: (query) => submitSearch(query),
)

notes: DsAvatar derives initials from a name or shows an image, with circle/square variants and size scopes.

propertyMappings.transform: Pass an image URL string instead of an ImageProvider
Provide the full name; initials are derived automatically
Use DsSize (sm/md/lg) instead of a pixel radius
DsAvatarVariant.circle (default) or .square

beforeCode:
CircleAvatar(
  backgroundImage: NetworkImage(url),
  child: Text('JD'),
)
afterCode:
DsAvatar(
  name: 'Jordan Doe',
  imageUrl: url,
)

notes: No direct Material equivalent. DsAvatarStack overlaps avatars and collapses overflow into a "+N" counter.

propertyMappings.transform: Pass DsAvatar widgets; overlap is handled automatically
Pixels of overlap between avatars
Avatars beyond this collapse into a +N badge

afterCode:
DsAvatarStack(
  maxVisible: 3,
  children: [
    DsAvatar(name: 'A B'),
    DsAvatar(name: 'C D'),
  ],
)

notes: DsAlert is a persistent inline message. Use data-color via severity (info/success/warning/danger). For transient messages a SnackBar is the Material analogue, but DsAlert is inline.

propertyMappings.transform: The severity selects the icon and color automatically
Compose action buttons inside the child

beforeCode:
MaterialBanner(
  content: Text('Saved'),
  leading: Icon(Icons.check),
  actions: [TextButton(onPressed: dismiss, child: Text('OK'))],
)
afterCode:
DsAlert(
  severity: DsSeverity.success,
  child: Text('Saved'),
)

notes: DsDetails is a keyboard-operable disclosure with a summary and collapsible content, with default/tinted variants.

propertyMappings.transform: A single child holds the collapsible content
DsDetailsVariant.default_ or .tinted

beforeCode:
ExpansionTile(
  title: Text('More'),
  children: [Text('Hidden content')],
)
afterCode:
DsDetails(
  summary: Text('More'),
  child: Text('Hidden content'),
)

notes: DsDropdown opens an overlay menu of items anchored to a trigger and reports the chosen value.

propertyMappings.transform: Provide a list of DsDropdownItem entries
The widget that opens the menu

beforeCode:
PopupMenuButton<String>(
  itemBuilder: (_) => [PopupMenuItem(value: 'a', child: Text('A'))],
  onSelected: handle,
  child: Icon(Icons.more_vert),
)
afterCode:
DsDropdown(
  trigger: Icon(DsIcons.menu),
  items: const [DsDropdownItem(value: 'a', label: 'A')],
  onSelected: handle,
)

notes: DsPopover anchors arbitrary content to a trigger with 12 placements, autoPlacement and controlled open state.

propertyMappings.transform: Any widget, not only menu items
Use DsPlacement instead of an offset
Controlled visibility

beforeCode:
MenuAnchor(
  builder: (_, ctrl, __) => IconButton(onPressed: ctrl.open, icon: Icon(Icons.info)),
  menuChildren: [Text('Details')],
)
afterCode:
DsPopover(
  placement: DsPlacement.bottom,
  trigger: Icon(DsIcons.info),
  content: Text('Details'),
)

notes: DsSuggestion is a combobox: a text field with a filterable option list, supporting multiple selection and creatable options.

propertyMappings.transform: Provide DsSuggestionOption list; filtering is built in
Reports the full selection list
Render selected values as removable chips
Allow creating new options from the query

beforeCode:
Autocomplete<String>(
  optionsBuilder: (v) => fruits.where((f) => f.contains(v.text)),
  onSelected: handle,
)
afterCode:
DsSuggestion<String>(
  options: const [DsSuggestionOption(value: 'a', label: 'Apple')],
  onSelectedChanged: (values) => handle(values),
)

notes: DsToggleGroup is a single-select segmented control with primary/secondary variants and arrow-key navigation.

propertyMappings.transform: A list of segment labels
Index of the selected segment
Reports the selected index
DsToggleGroupVariant.primary or .secondary

beforeCode:
SegmentedButton<int>(
  segments: const [ButtonSegment(value: 0, label: Text('Day')), ButtonSegment(value: 1, label: Text('Week'))],
  selected: {selected},
  onSelectionChanged: (s) => set(s.first),
)
afterCode:
DsToggleGroup(
  items: const ['Day', 'Week'],
  selectedIndex: selected,
  onChanged: set,
)

notes: DsInput is the bare single-line input (no label/affixes). For a labelled field use DsTextfield; for multi-line use DsTextarea.

propertyMappings.transform: Pass widgets directly

beforeCode:
TextField(
  controller: c,
  decoration: InputDecoration(hintText: 'Search'),
)
afterCode:
DsInput(
  controller: c,
  placeholder: 'Search',
)

notes: DsTextarea is a multi-line input built on DsInput; control the visible lines with rows.

propertyMappings.transform: Number of visible lines

beforeCode:
TextField(
  controller: c,
  maxLines: 4,
)
afterCode:
DsTextarea(
  controller: c,
  rows: 4,
)

notes: DsField wraps an input with a label, description and validation message (the React Field pattern).

propertyMappings.transform: Wrap any DsInput/DsSelect/DsCheckbox

beforeCode:
InputDecorator(
  decoration: InputDecoration(labelText: 'Email', errorText: err),
  child: child,
)
afterCode:
DsField(
  label: 'Email',
  error: err,
  child: child,
)

notes: No direct Material widget. DsFieldset groups related controls (e.g. radios) under a legend for correct grouping semantics.

propertyMappings.transform: Pass the legend widget

afterCode:
DsFieldset(
  legend: Text('Contact method'),
  children: [DsRadio(...), DsRadio(...)],
)

notes: No direct Material widget. DsErrorSummary lists form errors in a live region and links each to its field.

propertyMappings.transform: A list of error strings
Heading above the list
Focus/scroll to the related field

afterCode:
DsErrorSummary(
  title: 'Rett opp følgende:',
  errors: errors,
)

notes: DsLink is an inline text link with an underline and focus ring. Use onTap for navigation (Flutter idiom).

propertyMappings.transform: Pass the link text as a String

beforeCode:
TextButton(
  onPressed: open,
  child: Text('Read more'),
)
afterCode:
DsLink(
  onTap: open,
  text: 'Read more',
)

notes: No direct Material widget. DsBreadcrumbs renders a navigation trail; the last item is the current page (aria-current).

propertyMappings.transform: A list of label strings
Reports the tapped index

afterCode:
DsBreadcrumbs(
  items: const ['Home', 'Section', 'Current'],
  onItemTap: go,
)

notes: No direct Material widget. DsPagination renders page buttons with prev/next and an ellipsis for large ranges.

propertyMappings.transform: Max buttons before collapsing into an ellipsis

beforeCode: Row(children: List.generate(total, (i) => TextButton(...)))
afterCode:
DsPagination(
  currentPage: page,
  totalPages: 20,
  onPageChanged: go,
)

notes: DsTag is a small non-interactive label. For interactive chips use DsChip. Outline variant available.

propertyMappings.transform: Use a DsColor token
DsSelectionVariant.default_ or .outline

beforeCode: Chip(label: Text('New'))
afterCode: DsTag(child: Text('New'))

notes: No direct Material widget. DsSkipLink is an accessibility control that becomes visible on focus and jumps to main content.

propertyMappings.transform: Visible/announced text
Move focus to the main content target

beforeCode: // no Material equivalent
afterCode:
DsSkipLink(
  label: 'Hopp til hovedinnhold',
  onActivate: focusMain,
)

notes: DsHeading renders a semantic heading with the Designsystemet type scale and a level for screen readers.

propertyMappings.transform: Use DsHeadingLevel (xxl..xxs)

beforeCode: Text('Title', style: Theme.of(context).textTheme.headlineMedium)
afterCode: DsHeading(text: 'Title', level: DsHeadingLevel.lg)

notes: DsParagraph renders body text with the type scale (size + line-height variant).

propertyMappings.transform: DsBodySize + DsBodyVariant

beforeCode: Text('Lorem ipsum', style: Theme.of(context).textTheme.bodyMedium)
afterCode: DsParagraph(text: 'Lorem ipsum')

notes: DsLabel renders a form-control label with the correct weight and size.
beforeCode: Text('Email', style: TextStyle(fontWeight: FontWeight.w500))
afterCode: DsLabel(text: 'Email')

notes: DsValidationMessage renders an error (danger) or success message below a field.

propertyMappings.transform: true for danger (default), false for success

beforeCode: Text(err, style: TextStyle(color: Colors.red))
afterCode: DsValidationMessage(message: err)
